what_it_does: This file is a metadata attachment record that links a provider-registry channel-memory entry to the board item board://item-9b12c1af.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
It declares an attachment id att-feb721bd.
The target is a board:// URI pointing to item-9b12c1af.
An ISO 8601 added timestamp is present.
standouts: The target uses a board:// URI scheme (board://item-9b12c1af).
The attachment_type is board_items, indicating a cross-system link.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Stores attachments in a channel-memory/channel_attachments/ path.
References external board items with a URI-style target.
Records creation time as an ISO 8601 timestamp.
concerns: The file has no body or context explaining why the attachment exists.
The board:// target cannot be resolved from this file alone.
There is no author, source message, or update timestamp.
notes: A thin, timestamped bridge between a provider-registry channel memory record and a board item.
questions: What content or message in the provider-registry channel does this attachment relate to?
Why was this attachment created and what semantics does board_items imply?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is an attachment record and it records an attachment to a board item.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a durable, typed reference from a channel memory record to a board item so the two can be found together.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI as a target field for a board attachment is the distinctive detail.
contribution: It contributes a typed, timestamped edge between a channel-memory record and a board item, supporting traceability without duplicating either object's content.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-only channel-memory attachment record linking the provider-registry channel to the board item board://item-9b12c1af with a board_items attachment type.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to record that a provider-registry channel-memory item is attached to board item item-9b12c1af.
